Delta Hedge represents number of underlyings to buy/sell in order to hedge the position. Delta Hedge is not homegeneous and can not be aggregated across different underlyings  Delta Hedge is only available for options such as option on equity, option on commodity, FX option, option on futures contracts, option on bond, option on CDS, option on swap etc.
If currency pair is part of portfolio currency, Delta Home = Position Delta Ccy1 * Market FX rate; else 0.

Total sensitivity of a security to movements of the underlying price in dollar terms.Delta Notional represents the dollar amount needed for delta hedge.Sum(Delta Notional) = Sum(Delta Sensitivity* underlying price)

Change of market value in deal currency to unit change of underlying price. Delta Sensitivity represents sensitivity of deal value to the movement of underlying price. Delta Sensitivity is not homegeneous and can not be aggregated across different underlyings
Delta Sensitivity is only available for options such as option on equity, option on commodity, FX option, option on futures contracts, option on bond, option on CDS, option on swap etc.

Change of market value in underlying currency to unit change of underlying price.Delta represents number of underlyings to buy/sell in order to hedge the position.Delta is not homegeneous and can not be aggregated across different underlyings.Only available for deals with equity or index underlyings.
Primary information of the security including security type, notional, reference, etc

Total sensitivity of a security to 1 basis point decrease of the underlying interest rate curve. DV01 is available to all asset classes. For swaps, bonds and convertible bonds, option on bond and bond futures, option on interest rate futures, it is calculated as (downShiftValue-upShiftValue)/(2*shiftAmount) where shiftAmount is 10bp. For CDS, the shiftAmount is 1bp with the same formula.

Total sensitivity of a security to 1 basis point decrease of the underlying interest rate curve, expressed in terms of portfolio currency.Please see description for the 'DV01' field for more info.DV01 is available to all asset classes.For swaps, bonds and convertible bonds, option on bond and bond futures, option on interest rate futures, it is calculated as (downShiftValue-upShiftValue)/(2*shiftAmount) where shiftAmout is 10bp. For CDS, the shiftAmout is 1bp with the same formula.
For bonds the day a new security can be offered for sale.For swaps the date on which interest begins to accrue.

For Equity Call Options:Equity Notional = Position * Multiplier * Strike * 1For Equity Put Options: Equity Notional = Position * Multiplier * Strike * -1For securities with Equity underlyings:Equity Notional = Position * Multiplier * Market Price
Specifies exercise rights for Equity, Currency or Commodity derivatives: European, American or Bermudan.

The date on which the position expires and its payoff is finalized.For standard options this coincides with the last day (in case of American exercise style) or the only day (in case of European exercise style) on which the option may be exercised.

For Bonds/Loans/Par Traded Bonds: Contract Size * PositionFor OTC/CDS deals: Notional amount. For Cross Currency Swaps: Notional expressed in currency terms.For Short Term IR Futures/Options on IR Futures: Future price per number of position * delta (Value of 1.0pt).For Bond Futures: Notional * Underlying Price/100.For OTC Options on Bond futures: Market Value.For Commodity/Equity: Notional * Underlying Price.For FX deals: Notional. *Note: Face Amount currently not supported at deal level for multi-leg deals.

Intrinsic value in total position terms expressed in deal currency.The perceived current value of a security if it were to expire immediately, as opposed to itsmarket price which may also include a time value component.For a standard option if it is in the money, this value is calculated by determining the difference between the strike price and the underlying security's market price; otherwise it is 0.

Intrinsic value in total position terms expressed in portfolio currency.The perceived current value of a security if it were to expire immediately, as opposed to itsmarket price which may also include a time value component.For a standard option if it is in the money, this value is calculated by determining the difference between the strike price and the underlying security's market price; otherwise it is 0.

The prevailaing FX rate at the time of the valuation of the security to the portfolio.The FX rate between the currency of denomination of the security and the denomination currency for the portfolio.If the portfolio valuation date is set in the past, the FX rate is the closing price which follows the user settings on the contributor and the time reference zone chosen.

Quoted unit price of security in market quoting convention - Bonds and STN: par or unit price depending on the bond type. The price follows the bond's trading conventions (clean vs. dirty). - Equity: stock price as quoted on exchange. - Derivatives (Currency, Equity, Commodity, Short term interest rate Futures, Bond Futures): upfront premium. This is quoted on exchanges for listed options and modeled for OTC options. - OTC interest rate derivatives: upfront cost as % of notional. Marked to model. - CDS: upfront quoted or spread quoted. - Mortgages: % of original notional.  Loans: percent quoted.

Market Value of position expressed in its currency of denomination. Market Value represents the fair dollar value investors are willing to buy or sell, equivalent to present value of all future cash flows.  For clean quoted bonds, Market Value includes accrued interests. For futures, Market Value depends on 'Use zero cost for futures' user setting. When this setting is checked off, Market Value represents the money needed for the physical delivery of the underlying asset.  For mortgages, Market Value is adjusted to Mortgage Factor. For expired/matured/called deals, Market Value is 0.
Market value of the position, expressed in the first currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.
Market value of the position, expressed in the second currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.

Market Value of Position expressed in currency of denomination of the portfolio.The Market Value is converted by the relevant exchange rate at pricing date of the portfolio.For Bonds and Structured Notes this is calculated on dirty price (clean price plus accrued).

Percentage change of market value for 1% down shift of the underlying interest rate curve.Available for interest rates products. For deals other than swaps Modified Duration = 10000 * DV01/[abs(marketValue)], when marketValue is 0, this field will be blank.For swaps, it is calculated as [DV01 / (Notional + Market Value)] * 10000.

Total sensitivity of a security to 1% movements of the underlying price.Money Delta will be aggregated assuming all underlying price moved by 1%.Sum(Money Delta) = Sum(Delta Sensitivity * underlying price * 0.01)

For clean quoted security, Multiplier = (Market Value - accrued)/(Market Price).For dirty quoted security, Mulitplier = (Market Value)/(Market Price).Here the Market Value is for 1 position.For futures, Market Value in above formula is the Market Value with 'Use zero cost for futures' user setting Off;For non-linear security such as non-linear futures, Multiplier is not a constant, it is a function of price.

Option Adjusted Spread.OAS is a methodology using option pricing techniques to value the embedded options risk component of a bond's total spread.It applies to all bonds and structured notes; it is relative to the standard swap curve in the denomination currency of the bond. The OAS deployed in MARS is a 'stochastic' OAS, not the OAS to workout.For bonds without optionality OAS is equivalent to Z-Spread and represents the bond's constant spread over the benchmark zero coupon curve.

Price sensitivity per unit security to 1 unit movements of the underlying price.
Sensitivity of a security's Unit Delta to movements of the underlying price. Based on User Settings expressed in 1% movements or 1 currency unit movements of the underlying price.

Change of market value per unit underlying to 1% up shift of the underlying interest rate curve. For cross currency deals, it is the change of market value in deal currency to 1% up shift of the underlying interest rate curve. For example, unitRho = rho/100 for US listed option when contract size is 100.

The change of market value per unit underlying for a one day advance in the time to expiry. For equity derivatives, it is theta per underlying; for commodity, it is theta per underlying contract notional; for FX and interest rates product, it is theta per notional.
Vega sensitivity per unit security to 1% movements of the underlying price.

Change of market value to 1% increase of volatility per unit underlying. For equity derivatives, it is vega per underlying; for commodity, it is vega per underlying contract notional; for FX and interest rates product, it is vega per notional.
Volga sensitivity per unit security to 1% absolute changes in volatility
Sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% movements of the underlying price.Applies to Equity, Currency and Commodity derivatives.

Vanna of the position, expressed in the first currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.Vanna expresses the sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% movements of the underlying price.

Vanna of the position, expressed in the second currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.Vanna expresses the sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% movements of the underlying price.

Change of market value to 1% increase of volatility. Supported for options, convertible bonds and interest rates products. For options on stock, index, warrants, currency spot, commodity futures or currency futures, volatility is derived from underlying stock price, index price, warrants price, currency spot, commodity futures price or currency futures price. For option on interest rate futures and option on bonds, if calculation is yield based, volatility is derived from yield; if calculation is price based, volatility is derived from futures or bond price. For convertible bonds, volatility is derived from underlying stock price. For bonds with embedded options and swaps, volatility is the interest rate volatility.

Implied volatility calculated on market price expressed in %.For OTC derivatives usually interpolated volatility from a market volatility surface.For underlyings where no market surface is available historical volatility is used.For listed options where market prices are available the volatility is calculated (implied) out of the quoted market option price.For interest rate volatility, it could either be normal vol or log-normal vol based on the Volatility Type column.
Sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% absolute changes in Volatility.Sometimes this is also referred to as Vomma. Applies to Equity, Currency and Commodity derivatives.

Volga of the position, expressed in the first currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.Volga (sometimes also called Vomma) expresses the sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% absolute changes in Volatility.

Volga of the position, expressed in the second currency of the currency pair.It applies exclusively to currency deals.Volga (sometimes also called Vomma) expresses the sensitivity of a security's total vega to 1% absolute changes in Volatility.
Specifies the type of volatility used in the current calculation of prices and sensitivities.
